placeholders.{BLOCK_NAME}: tab-list (kebab-case — block name, file names, JSON IDs, CSS class prefix, all derived classes like -wrapper, -fade-left, -fade-right, -container)
placeholders.{BLOCK_PANEL}: tab-panel (kebab-case — paired panel component name, JSON IDs, model/filter references)
placeholders.{BLOCK_FUNC}: Tab List (title-case — display titles in Storybook, JSON template names)
placeholders.{BLOCK_PANEL_FUNC}: Tab Panel (title-case — display title for the paired panel component in JSON)

pairedComponentNote._important: This block uses a PAIRED COMPONENT pattern. The 'tab-list' block provides the navigation UI, while 'tab-panel' is a section-level component (resourceType: section) that holds the content for each tab. When renaming, derive {BLOCK_PANEL} from {BLOCK_NAME} by replacing '-list' with '-panel' (e.g., 'nav-tabs' → 'nav-panel'; 'pill-list' → 'pill-panel').

preservedPatterns._note: ARIA tab roles, camelCase JS variables, and the 'tab-label' data attribute are NOT replaced with placeholders because they reference the generic tab UI concept (WAI-ARIA Tabs pattern), not the block name.

derivedClasses._note: All CSS classes are derived from {BLOCK_NAME} by appending suffixes. No separate placeholders needed.

replacements.context: block wrapper class, file names, JSON block ID/model, all -wrapper/-fade-*/-container derived classes
panel section JSON definition ID, model, filter, components array

replacements.note: NOT replaced — WAI-ARIA standard tab pattern roles
NOT replaced — JS variable names using generic tab concept
NOT replaced — section-metadata field name and HTML data attribute
NOT replaced — EDS icon system classes
NOT replaced — global design system classes referenced in panel CSS
